articles.title: Google just gave Sundar Pichai a $692M pay package
articles.description: Most of it is tied to performance, including new stock incentives linked to Waymo and Wing, its drone delivery venture.

digest:
## 🔬 Major Technical Developments

### Agent Safehouse – macOS-native sandboxing for local agents
URL: https://agent-safehouse.dev/
First serious attempt at OS-level containment for autonomous AI agents
As agents gain filesystem and network access, sandboxing becomes critical infrastructure. This addresses the elephant in the room: letting LLMs execute code locally without handing them the keys to everything. Watch for enterprise security teams to mandate similar tooling within 12 months.

### Living human brain cells play DOOM on a CL1
URL: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yRV8fSw6HaE
Biological compute moves from research curiosity to demonstration phase
CorticalLabs continues pushing organoid computing into mainstream visibility. The DOOM benchmark is a meme, but the underlying capability—training biological neural networks for task completion—has serious long-term implications for compute paradigms beyond silicon.

### We should revisit literate programming in the agent era
URL: https://silly.business/blog/we-should-revisit-literate-programming-in-the-agent-era/
Code-as-prose may finally find its moment as agents become primary consumers
Knuth's 40-year-old idea gains new relevance when the reader is an LLM, not a human. Agent-readable codebases could become a competitive moat. Tooling companies should take note.

## 💼 Business & Market Signals

### Palmer Luckey's retro gaming startup ModRetro reportedly seeks funding at $1B valuation
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/08/palmer-luckeys-retro-gaming-startup-modretro-reportedly-seeks-funding-at-1b-valuation/
Hardware nostalgia play hits unicorn territory 18 months post-launch
Chromatic shipped in 2024; $1B ask in 2026 implies either exceptional sell-through or Luckey premium. Retro gaming hardware is a $2B+ segment growing 15% annually. The real question: is this a product company or a platform play for the IP licensing deals?

### Will the Pentagon's Anthropic controversy scare startups away from defense work?
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/08/will-the-pentagons-anthropic-controversy-scare-startups-away-from-defense-work/
Defense-tech deal flow may bifurcate into "clean" and "classified" startup cohorts
The chilling effect is real but asymmetric. Companies with consumer-facing brands face reputational risk; pure-play defense startups see reduced competition. Palantir and Anduril benefit from the sorting. Expect talent to stratify similarly.

### Google just gave Sundar Pichai a $692M pay package
URL: https://techcrunch.com/2026/03/07/google-just-gave-sundar-pichai-a-692m-pay-package/
Waymo and Wing equity kickers signal where Alphabet sees alpha
Stock incentives tied to specific subsidiaries rather than core search is a governance tell. The board is betting the robotaxi and drone delivery units justify premium multiples. Waymo valuation floor implied north of $50B based on these structures.

## 📋 Case Studies & Deep Dives

### Getting started with ClickHouse? 13 mistakes and how to avoid them
URL: https://clickhouse.com/blog/common-getting-started-issues-with-clickhouse
Vendor documentation that doubles as competitive moat
ClickHouse continues aggressive developer education push against Snowflake and Databricks. The 13-item listicle format is deliberate SEO play. Real-time analytics infrastructure remains a $4B+ TAM with fragmented winners.

### Linux Internals: How /proc/self/mem writes to unwritable memory
URL: https://offlinemark.com/an-obscure-quirk-of-proc/
Security-relevant kernel behavior that most engineers don't understand
This 2021 deep dive resurfaces as AI agents increasingly interact with Linux systems at low levels. The /proc/self/mem quirk has implications for sandboxing, debugging, and exploit development. Essential reading for anyone building agent containment.

### Orchestration for zero-human companies
URL: https://paperclip.ing
The logical endpoint of agent-first architecture, now productized
Fully autonomous company orchestration is the vibe-coding thesis taken to its conclusion. Whether this is vaporware or real infrastructure matters less than the fact that serious people are building toward this future. Due diligence on any AI-native startup should include "how does this break if humans leave the loop?"

## ⚡ Signal vs. Noise

The through-line today is containment versus capability. Agent sandboxing, zero-human orchestration, and Pentagon AI controversies all orbit the same question: how much autonomy do we grant systems we cannot fully audit? The money is flowing toward capability while infrastructure for control lags behind. For PE, the play is picks-and-shovels in the safety stack—sandboxing, observability, kill switches. The companies building guardrails will be acqui-hired or mandated by regulators within 24 months.
